hero.title: Your Guide to the Democratic Process
hero.description: Get answers to your election questions in your preferred language. Learn about voting, candidates, and the democratic process with AI-powered assistance.

chat.title: AI-Powered Chat
chat.description: Ask questions about elections, voting rights, and the democratic process in any of 8 supported languages.

timeline.title: Election Timeline
timeline.description: Understand every stage of the election process from voter registration to inauguration.

checklist.title: Voter Checklist
checklist.description: Track your voter readiness with an interactive checklist personalized to your needs.

multilingual.title: 8 Languages
multilingual.description: Full support for English, Hindi, Spanish, French, Arabic, Bengali, Portuguese, and Mandarin.

chat.title: Chat with ElectionBot
chat.welcome: Hello! I'm ElectionBot, your civic education assistant. Ask me anything about elections, voting, and the democratic process!
chat.error: Sorry, I encountered an error. Please try again.
chat.offTopic: I appreciate your question, but I'm designed to help with election and civic education topics. Could you ask me something about voting, elections, or the democratic process?

quickChips.howToRegister: How do I register to vote?
quickChips.primaryVsCaucus: What's the difference between a primary and a caucus?

timeline.title: Election Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Understanding the journey from candidacy to inauguration

announcement.title: Candidate Announcement
announcement.description: Candidates formally announce their intention to run for office. They begin building campaign teams, raising funds, and establishing their platform on key issues.
announcement.details: During this phase, candidates file official paperwork with the Federal Election Commission (FEC), form exploratory committees, and begin courting donors and supporters. Media coverage intensifies as the field of candidates takes shape.

primaries.title: Primaries & Caucuses
primaries.description: Voters in each state choose their preferred candidate through primary elections or caucuses. Delegates are awarded based on results.
primaries.details: Primaries are state-run elections where voters cast secret ballots. Caucuses are local gatherings where participants openly show support. The Iowa caucuses and New Hampshire primary traditionally kick off the process. Candidates need a majority of delegates to secure the nomination.

conventions.title: National Conventions
conventions.description: Each major party holds a national convention to formally nominate their presidential candidate and adopt a party platform.
conventions.details: Delegates from each state vote to officially nominate the presidential candidate. The candidate then selects a vice presidential running mate. The convention features speeches, platform adoption, and generates significant media attention.

generalCampaign.title: General Election Campaign
generalCampaign.description: Nominated candidates campaign across the country, participate in debates, and work to win over voters in key battleground states.
generalCampaign.details: Presidential debates are organized by the Commission on Presidential Debates. Candidates focus on swing states where the outcome is uncertain. Campaign advertising, rallies, and grassroots organizing intensify as Election Day approaches.

electionDay.title: Election Day
electionDay.description: Citizens cast their votes at polling places across the country. Some states also allow early voting and mail-in ballots.
electionDay.details: Polls are open for specified hours determined by each state. Voters may need to show identification depending on state laws. Results are reported throughout the evening as polls close across time zones.

electoralVote.title: Electoral College Vote
electoralVote.description: Electors from each state formally cast their votes for president and vice president based on the popular vote results in their state.
electoralVote.details: Each state has a number of electors equal to its total congressional delegation. Most states use a winner-take-all system. A candidate needs 270 of 538 electoral votes to win. Electors meet in their respective state capitals.

certification.title: Congressional Certification
certification.description: Congress meets in a joint session to count and certify the electoral votes, officially declaring the winner of the presidential election.
certification.details: The Vice President presides over the joint session. Electoral votes are counted state by state. Members of Congress can object to specific state results, requiring written objections from both a House member and a Senator.

inauguration.title: Inauguration
inauguration.description: The newly elected president takes the oath of office and begins their term. The ceremony takes place at the U.S. Capitol.
inauguration.details: The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court administers the oath of office. The new president delivers an inaugural address outlining their vision and priorities. The ceremony includes prayers, musical performances, and a parade.

checklist.title: Voter Readiness Checklist
checklist.subtitle: Make sure you're ready to vote. Track your progress below.

registration.title: Register to Vote
registration.description: Check your voter registration status or register for the first time at your state's election website.

id.title: Verify Your ID
id.description: Make sure you have a valid form of identification accepted by your state for voting purposes.

pollingPlace.title: Find Your Polling Place
pollingPlace.description: Look up your designated polling location so you know exactly where to go on Election Day.

researchCandidates.title: Research Candidates
researchCandidates.description: Learn about the candidates and ballot measures you'll be voting on. Review their positions on key issues.

absentee.title: Request Absentee Ballot (if needed)
absentee.description: If you can't vote in person, request an absentee or mail-in ballot before the deadline.

sampleBallot.title: Review Sample Ballot
sampleBallot.description: Get familiar with the ballot format and all the races and measures that will appear.

transportPlan.title: Plan Your Transportation
transportPlan.description: Figure out how you'll get to your polling place. Many communities offer free rides on Election Day.

voteReminder.title: Set a Voting Reminder
voteReminder.description: Set calendar reminders for registration deadlines, early voting periods, and Election Day.

about.title: About ElectionBot

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: ElectionBot is a multilingual civic education assistant designed to help voters understand the democratic process. We believe that informed citizens are the foundation of a healthy democracy.

technology.title: Technology
technology.description: Built with Google Gemini AI for intelligent conversation, Google Translate API for real-time translation, and modern web technologies for a seamless experience.

languages.title: Supported Languages
languages.description: ElectionBot supports 8 languages to reach diverse communities: English, Hindi, Spanish, French, Arabic, Bengali, Portuguese, and Mandarin Chinese.

about.disclaimer: ElectionBot provides educational information only. Always verify official election information with your local election authority.

services.title: Google Services
services.fonts: Noto font family for all scripts
